calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2026 Continued To Jul-22-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
Joshua Falakassa, Esq. 818-456-6168 Falakassa Law for Plaintiff.
Afshin Najaf, Esq. 1-415-732-1182 for Defendant Continental Casualty Company (In-person)
Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909.831.2644, csrjane@hotmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received. The Court takes a 2 minutes recess for Mr. Falakassa to wear a tie.

9:17am Court is back in session. The Court admonishes parties re: standard of conduct.

ARGUED.

Mr. Najafi contests and presents arguments; raise issue on 2nd cause of action - unfair competition law. Raise three points; civil penalties not available to PLaintiff, Plaintiff has no standing under unfair competition law, failed to plead viable UCL remedy.

Court comments; give notice required under code that non compete clause are unenforceable.

Mr. Falakassas agrees with the TR, issue on prayer for relief.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Continental Casualty Insurance' Demurrer to Complaint.

Taken Under Submission. ***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909.831.2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Continued To Jun-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

2 - DEFENDANT CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's Demurrer is deemed moot, in light of FAC filed February 5, 2026. Prevailing party to provide proposed order for the Court's review. Prevailing party to drop a hard copy to Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: John M. True, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMT)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Defendant's Motion To Quash Service of Summons is GRANTED.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and drop a hard copy to Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: John M. True, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMT)
